message:
chore(beads): close beads_rust-ils3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -462,7 +462,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-i9fd","title":"Add schema document goldens for JSON and TOON schema output","description":"Testing posture: golden artifacts

Finding: br schema all --format json and br schema all --format toon are advertised as agent/tooling integration documents. They need frozen schema-document goldens so accidental changes to target names, field definitions, descriptions, nesting, or TOON folding are reviewed intentionally.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add golden artifacts for br schema all --format json and br schema all --format toon.
- Normalize only generated_at or other explicitly volatile metadata; keep schema content, key order, target names, and TOON structure exact.
- Add tests that decode the TOON golden and compare it with the JSON schema semantics where practical, while still preserving a textual TOON golden for output stability.
- Include a documented update workflow for intentional schema/document changes.
- Ensure failures clearly show artifact diffs suitable for code review.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T21:51:49.735457086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:25:31.102086319Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:25:31.101698663Z","close_reason":"Added JSON and TOON schema document goldens for schema all output.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":292,"issue_id":"beads_rust-i9fd","author":"ubuntu","text":"degraded-coordination: Agent Mail health is red; intended edit surface is tests/snapshots/** plus generated schema JSON/TOON insta snapshots.","created_at":"2026-04-21T22:18:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-ik2a","title":"E2E completions: add per-test logging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T21:41:46.305084031Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T21:44:18.120659112Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T21:44:18.120659112Z","close_reason":"Added per-test logging and init_test_logging to tests/e2e_completions.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-ik2a","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-ile","title":"Unit tests: storage deps/labels/comments/events","description":"# Storage Relations

## Focus
- Dependency insert/remove, cycles, and metadata.
- Labels add/remove/list and label filters.
- Comments insert/list and ordering.
- Events correctness + ordering + counts.

## Notes
- Use real SQLite and actual DB rows.
- Include orphan/invalid cases to confirm errors.","notes":"Added storage relation tests in src/storage/sqlite.rs (labels add/remove sorted, dependency add/remove + cycle check, comments ordering).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T16:24:14.719914036Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T16:42:57.546693784Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T16:42:57.546693784Z","close_reason":"Added relation tests for deps/labels/comments in storage/sqlite.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
-{"id":"beads_rust-ils3","title":"[P2] Incremental blocked-cache update allocates affected IDs then deletes row-by-row","description":"Root cause: src/storage/sqlite.rs:3321-3333 converts the whole affected HashSet into a Vec only to call chunks(), then deletes each affected blocked_issues_cache row one at a time. On a large affected parent-child component this adds an avoidable O(n) allocation and O(n) DELETE statements inside the write path. The surrounding incremental blocked-cache logic is meant to avoid full rebuild cost, so this path can still bottleneck large dependency edits or imports that touch broad components.

Repro: create a workspace with a 10k-node dependency/parent-child component, mutate a blocker near the root, and profile the blocked-cache update. benches/storage_perf.rs already has Ready (10k issues / 20k deps) coverage; add a mutation/dependency-update variant to expose this delete phase.

Proposed fix: avoid collecting solely for chunking by streaming affected IDs through a small reusable buffer, or use a temporary table / parameterized IN delete per bounded chunk once fsqlite supports the path safely. Keep row-level fallback only for the known fsqlite bug case and document the threshold.

Test/bench to add: add a Criterion benchmark around dependency mutation or blocked-cache refresh for a large component, with query-count/latency expectations tied to the existing storage_perf readiness benchmarks.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:16:04.229737339Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:17:29.730738962Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"beads_rust-ils3","title":"[P2] Incremental blocked-cache update allocates affected IDs then deletes row-by-row","description":"Root cause: src/storage/sqlite.rs:3321-3333 converts the whole affected HashSet into a Vec only to call chunks(), then deletes each affected blocked_issues_cache row one at a time. On a large affected parent-child component this adds an avoidable O(n) allocation and O(n) DELETE statements inside the write path. The surrounding incremental blocked-cache logic is meant to avoid full rebuild cost, so this path can still bottleneck large dependency edits or imports that touch broad components.

Repro: create a workspace with a 10k-node dependency/parent-child component, mutate a blocker near the root, and profile the blocked-cache update. benches/storage_perf.rs already has Ready (10k issues / 20k deps) coverage; add a mutation/dependency-update variant to expose this delete phase.

Proposed fix: avoid collecting solely for chunking by streaming affected IDs through a small reusable buffer, or use a temporary table / parameterized IN delete per bounded chunk once fsqlite supports the path safely. Keep row-level fallback only for the known fsqlite bug case and document the threshold.

Test/bench to add: add a Criterion benchmark around dependency mutation or blocked-cache refresh for a large component, with query-count/latency expectations tied to the existing storage_perf readiness benchmarks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:16:04.229737339Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:20:09.232896642Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:20:09.232625835Z","close_reason":"Committed 228fc2d: batched DELETE...WHERE IN replaces row-by-row loop. 106 storage tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-imc8","title":"Fuzz JSONL import with malformed and adversarial records","description":"Testing-fuzzing follow-up. Add a cargo-fuzz/libFuzzer target for the JSONL import surface.

Scope:
- Feed arbitrary byte streams and line-oriented malformed/adversarial JSONL into the import path that exercises import_from_jsonl/import-only semantics without touching the developer workspace.
- Seed corpus with truncated JSON, duplicate IDs, invalid UTF-8 boundaries after lossy conversion decisions, oversized fields, conflicting dependency/comment/event shapes, tombstones, unknown fields, old-schema records, and mixed valid/invalid lines.
- Assert no panic, no process abort, no hangs, and no partial data loss in a temp isolated storage directory.
- Expected failures must surface as structured errors or rejected records; successful imports must preserve valid records and storage invariants.
- Document how to run the target locally and in CI-friendly bounded mode.

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo-fuzz target exists for JSONL import.
- Harness uses temp storage and never reads/writes the real .beads directory.
- Invariants check issue count/hash consistency and absence of dangling dependency/comment rows after each run.
- Regression seeds are checked in under the fuzz corpus.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistyDune","created_at":"2026-04-21T21:51:01.615143814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:16:59.225503520Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:16:59.225170566Z","close_reason":"Added cargo-fuzz JSONL import harness with isolated temp storage, invariant checks, seed corpus, and run docs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":291,"issue_id":"beads_rust-imc8","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented cargo-fuzz jsonl_import harness under fuzz/fuzz_targets with isolated temp .beads storage, sentinel rollback checks for failed imports, relation/integrity invariants after imports, seed corpus for malformed/duplicate/conflict/tombstone/relation cases, and fuzz README run instructions. Verification: cargo fmt --check, cargo fuzz check jsonl_import, cargo fuzz run jsonl_import -- -runs=1 -max_len=65536, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, fuzz package clippy, and ubs on fuzz files via rch/local fallback as needed.","created_at":"2026-04-21T22:16:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-iqj9","title":"Fix routed show --format json/toon output context","description":"Fresh-eye audit found src/cli/commands/show.rs uses the outer/global OutputContext when rendering routed multi-project results for --format json or --format toon. In TTY mode that can render rich JSON instead of plain JSON, and in plain mode it can emit nothing at all because OutputContext::json_pretty/toon only write when the context mode matches. The routed branch should render with a command-scoped OutputContext derived from the resolved output format, matching the non-routed path. Add regression coverage for routed JSON/TOON format requests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:38:33.686600050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T16:28:16.442596455Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T16:28:16.441857351Z","close_reason":"Already fixed: routed show path at lines 129 and 187 already creates command-scoped OutputContext via from_output_format() instead of using outer_ctx. Fix was implemented in commits 9b43240 and b1b9d67.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-ir0t","title":"Scenario DSL + normalization rules for conformance","description":"Define a scenario registry/DSL so each test case can drive E2E, conformance, and benchmark modes from the same script.

Scope
- Scenario struct: setup (dataset or fresh), commands (argv + env), expected invariants, and expected JSON shapes.
- Normalization rules for conformance: stable sorting, ignore volatile fields (timestamps, randomized ids) where needed, and compare semantics (counts, statuses, deps).
- Shared assertions for “no git ops”, path confinement, and deterministic JSON output.

Acceptance
- Scenarios can be executed in modes: E2E (br only), Conformance (br vs bd), Benchmark (timing + RSS).
- Normalization is explicit and logged when applied.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueStream","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:40:47.932670110Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T04:32:24.020248596Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T04:32:24.020248596Z","close_reason":"Scenario DSL complete: Scenario, ScenarioCommand, ScenarioResult, ScenarioRunner, NormalizationRules, ScenarioFilter all implemented with E2E/Conformance/Benchmark modes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-ir0t","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-7wqg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-ir0t","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ag35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":151,"issue_id":"beads_rust-ir0t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Normalization guidance: for read-only commands expect byte-for-byte JSON equality after stable sort; for mutating commands compare structural invariants (status/priority/labels/deps) and allow timestamp skew. Log any field-level ignore so conformance diffs remain explainable.","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:40:53Z"}]}
